notes: All checks pass. The original_text "Microsoft Cloud revenue increased 23% to $168.9 billion." is confirmed verbatim from the fiscal year 2025 highlights section of the Microsoft 10-K (accession 0000950170-25-100235). Total company revenue was $281.724 billion, up $36.6 billion or 15% — not 23%. The $168.9 billion figure belongs exclusively to the Microsoft Cloud business segment. The modified_text/claim correctly swaps the subject to "total company revenue," which is the scope_expansion hallucination. The citation is intact and accurate. expected_verdict of partially_supported is correct for a scope_expansion type (figures are real but attributed to the wrong scope). Subtlety rating of 4 is plausible: the dollar amount and growth rate are accurate, only the subject entity is changed, making it easy to miss without cross-checking segment vs. total figures.
haiku_reason: The claim contains specific numbers, cites an official regulatory document (Form 10-K with specific fiscal period), and presents figures that are plausible for Microsoft's scale and growth trajectory. A 23% growth rate is high but not implausible, and $168.9 billion is a realistic revenue figure. Without access to the source, there are no self-evident red flags that would indicate misrepresentation—no obvious logical contradictions, implausibly round numbers, or consistency issues.
